steps.title: Find the predicted pattern
steps.content: Patel's hypothesis says otter damage increases eelgrass sexual reproduction, which increases genetic diversity and improves meadow health. That predicts healthier eelgrass where otter populations are larger and longer established.

steps.title: Reject weak challenges
steps.content:
• Choice A describes small, recently reintroduced populations and does not contradict the predicted pattern.
  • Choice B discusses sites with no eelgrass meadows, so it cannot compare eelgrass health.
  • Choice D concerns plants unrelated to eelgrass.
The remaining choice reverses the predicted relationship by linking larger, longer-established otter populations with worse eelgrass health. The answer is C.
